what: Prepare a Check Result for Plotting — as_plot_data
title: Prepare a Check Result for Plotting — as_plot_data
text: Coerces check object — result check_na_timing() (anicheck package) — plot-ready data frame plot() method draws . staging step sits diagnostic computation (check_*() function) ggplot: reshapes natural, analysis-friendly columns check exact aesthetic contract geom needs (single grouping factor, ordered fill levels, right level ordering, ).

title: Prepare a Check Result for Plotting — as_plot_data
code: as_plot_data(x, ...)  # Default S3 method as_plot_data(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'check_confidence' as_plot_data(x, ..., clip = 0.02)  # S3 method for class 'check_na_gapsize' as_plot_data(   x,   ...,   ranked_by = c("occurrence", "total"),   limit = 10,   include_total = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'check_na_timing' as_plot_data(x, ..., measure = c("percent", "count"), n_intervals = NULL)

title: Prepare a Check Result for Plotting — as_plot_data
text: x check object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. clip as_plot_data.check_confidence(): density floor (fraction keypoint's peak) violin cut. Default 0.02. ranked_by, limit, include_total as_plot_data.check_na_gapsize(): order bars "occurrence" "total", keep top limit per group, whether include total-NAs series. measure as_plot_data.check_na_timing(): "percent" (default, share interval) "count" (number frames). n_intervals as_plot_data.check_na_timing(): number time intervals bin (default NULL uses Sturges' rule largest group).

title: Prepare a Check Result for Plotting — as_plot_data
text: data frame classed corresponding plot method (example anivis_check_na_timing_data).

title: Prepare a Check Result for Plotting — as_plot_data
text: anivis analog data_plot() see package. users never call directly — plot() calls . Reach want precise rows plot built , inspect assemble custom chart hand. Methods exist check objects single canonical plot (one object class maps one figure). Object types can plotted several different ways — aniframe, feeds plot_trajectory() plot_timeseries() — deliberately single method, since nothing dispatch choose shapes. as_plot_data.check_confidence() turns per-keypoint density grid closed violin polygons: keypoint sits integer y position, density mirrored either side along confidence (x) axis (width-normalised every violin maximum width). Grid points clip x peak dropped, thin tails bimodal-bridging necks disappear split distribution becomes separate polygons. keypoint x-axis category; identity varies (e.g. individual) collapses group factor faceting. x positions, per-keypoint median / quartile overlay, facet ride along attributes. Returns frame classed anivis_check_confidence_data. as_plot_data.check_na_gapsize() reshapes gap-size table long, ranked form imputeTS-style bar chart needs: one row per (group, gap size, series), series occurrence (total include_total), value count, key reorder_within-style factor ("<n> NA-gap___ <group>") ordered ranked_by facet sorts independently. Returns frame classed anivis_check_na_gapsize_data. as_plot_data.check_na_timing() reconstructs, compact gap table, missing / present frame counts per time interval — one row per (group, interval, status). single interval width (frames) chosen groups (Sturges' rule default) bars line across panels, gap's overlap interval counted, per-frame data needed. value share (measure = "percent") count ("count"), width interval's span time units, frame interval size rides along attribute. Returns frame classed anivis_check_na_timing_data.

what: Draw point events as dots or raster ticks — geom_event_point
title: Draw point events as dots or raster ticks — geom_event_point
text: ggplot2 layer renders instantaneous events event's start time, one row per label (channel, ...) y axis. Two styles available via style:

title: Draw point events as dots or raster ticks — geom_event_point
code: geom_event_point(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   ...,   style = c("point", "raster"),   size = 1.8,   height = 0.7,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Draw point events as dots or raster ticks — geom_event_point
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Merged top defaults, need supply aesthetics want override. data data frame (typically anievent). NULL, inherits parent ggplot() call. Either way, rows type != "point" dropped layer. style Either "point" (default, dot per event) "raster" (vertical tick per event). size Dot size, style = "point". Default 1.8. height Tick height y-axis units (1 = spacing adjacent rows), style = "raster". Default 0.7. na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes, ... Passed ggplot2::layer().

title: Draw point events as dots or raster ticks — geom_event_point
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Draw point events as dots or raster ticks — geom_event_point
text: "point" (default) — dot event time. "raster" — short vertical tick event time, classic spike-raster look (standard e.g. neuron spike trains). input type column (e.g. aniframe::anievent()), rows type != "point" dropped — point half state/point pair complementing geom_event_state(). default mapping aes(x = start) — every aesthetic caller. Common patterns: aes(colour = label) — colour mark behaviour. aes(y = label) (y = channel, y = subject, ...) — spread marks across rows. y mapping marks collapse onto single line. Opinionated wrappers (themes, colour scales, sensible defaults) intended live one layer ; geom deliberately bare. thin wrapper around ggplot2::GeomPoint ("point") ggplot2::GeomSegment ("raster"), setup_data step supplies default y none mapped.

what: Draw state events as horizontal bars — geom_event_state
title: Draw state events as horizontal bars — geom_event_state
text: ggplot2 layer renders state (durative) events rectangles spanning event's start -> stop x axis, one row per label y axis. input type column (e.g. aniframe::anievent()), rows type != "state" dropped.

title: Draw state events as horizontal bars — geom_event_state
code: geom_event_state(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   ...,   height = 0.7,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Draw state events as horizontal bars — geom_event_state
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Merged top defaults, need supply aesthetics want override. data data frame (typically anievent). NULL, inherits parent ggplot() call. Either way, rows type != "state" dropped layer. height Vertical extent bout bar, y-axis units (1 = spacing adjacent label rows). Default 0.7. na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes, ... Passed ggplot2::layer().

title: Draw state events as horizontal bars — geom_event_state
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Draw state events as horizontal bars — geom_event_state
text: default mapping aes(xmin = start, xmax = stop) — every aesthetic caller. Common patterns: aes(fill = label) — colour bout's interior behaviour. aes(colour = label) — outline bout (keeps sub-pixel-wide bouts visible zoom). aes(y = label) (y = channel, y = subject, ...) — spread bouts across rows. y mapping bouts collapse onto single line. + ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) — split channels panels. Opinionated wrappers (themes, colour scales, sensible defaults) intended live one layer ; geom deliberately bare. thin wrapper around ggplot2::GeomRect: subclass adds setup_data step derives ymin / ymax y + height, discrete y scale keeps label rows ranked correctly (including per-panel scales = "free_y").

what: Plot the Distribution of Tracking Confidence — plot.anivis_check_confidence
title: Plot the Distribution of Tracking Confidence — plot.anivis_check_confidence
text: Renders check_confidence() result (anicheck package) horizontal violin per keypoint tracking confidence (keypoints y axis, confidence x), point median line spanning inter-quartile range. violin sitting far right tight reliably tracked keypoint; left-shifted body long low tail flags one tracker often unsure . several individuals, gets panel.

title: Plot the Distribution of Tracking Confidence — plot.anivis_check_confidence
code: # S3 method for class 'anivis_check_confidence' plot(x, ..., clip = 0.02, mode = c("light", "dark"))

title: Plot the Distribution of Tracking Confidence — plot.anivis_check_confidence
text: x check_confidence object (anicheck package). ... Additional arguments (currently unused). clip Cut violin density falls fraction peak, thin tails neck bridging bimodal distribution removed violin shows data actually . Default 0.02; set 0 keep full density. mode Either "light" (default) "dark"; passed theme_imputets().

title: Plot the Distribution of Tracking Confidence — plot.anivis_check_confidence
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot the Distribution of Tracking Confidence — plot.anivis_check_confidence
text: violins drawn kernel-density grid stored check object (via geom_polygon), raw values needed. Styling matches check plots (theme_imputets(), horizontal-gridlines). plot built intermediate frame class anivis_check_confidence_data produced as_plot_data() — staging step mirrors data_plot() see.

what: Plot the Occurrence of Missing-Value Gap Sizes — plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize
title: Plot the Occurrence of Missing-Value Gap Sizes — plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize
text: Renders check_na_gapsize() result (anicheck package) horizontal bar chart often gap size occurs — adaptation ggplot_na_gapsize() imputeTS package (Moritz & Gatscha, GPL-3). every gap length draws number gaps (indianred) , optionally, total missing frames account (steelblue, gap size times count). Bars ranked common (costly) gaps sit top; top limit shown. one group, gets panel.

title: Plot the Occurrence of Missing-Value Gap Sizes — plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize
code: # S3 method for class 'anivis_check_na_gapsize' plot(   x,   ...,   ranked_by = c("occurrence", "total"),   limit = 10,   include_total = TRUE,   mode = c("light", "dark") )

title: Plot the Occurrence of Missing-Value Gap Sizes — plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize
text: x check_na_gapsize object (anicheck package). ... Additional arguments (currently unused). ranked_by Order bars "occurrence" (default, gap frequency) "total" (resulting missing frames). limit Maximum number gap sizes (bars) show per group, keeping top-ranked. Default 10. include_total Whether add steelblue “resulting NAs” bar alongside occurrence bar. Default TRUE. mode Either "light" (default) "dark"; passed theme_animovement().

title: Plot the Occurrence of Missing-Value Gap Sizes — plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize
text: ggplot object.

what: Plot State and Point Events — plot_events
title: Plot State and Point Events — plot_events
text: Creates ggplot overlays state events (durative bouts, drawn horizontal bars geom_event_state()) point events (instantaneous occurrences, drawn dots geom_event_point()) shared time axis. behaviour label becomes row y axis.

title: Plot State and Point Events — plot_events
code: plot_events(data, ...)  # S3 method for class 'anievent' plot_events(   data,   ...,   layout = c("facet", "inline"),   point_style = c("point", "raster"),   mode = c("light", "dark") )  # Default S3 method plot_events(   data = NULL,   point = NULL,   ...,   point_style = c("point", "raster"),   mode = c("light", "dark") )

title: Plot State and Point Events — plot_events
text: data anievent object, data frame state events default method. ... Additional arguments (currently unused). layout plot_events.anievent(): either "facet" (default, one y row per label channels faceted) "inline" (ethogram — one y row per channel labels colour legend). point_style point events drawn: "point" (default, dots) "raster" (vertical ticks, classic spike-raster look). Passed geom_event_point(). mode Either "light" (default) "dark"; passed theme_animovement(). point default method : optional data frame point events. Ignored plot_events.anievent().

title: Plot State and Point Events — plot_events
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot State and Point Events — plot_events
text: Two dispatches: plot_events.anievent() — auto-detects rows states points reading type column. layout argument chooses channels labels arranged: "facet" (default): label y-axis row, multiple channels become facet rows (free y-scales). variables_what column one level adds facet column per identity — facet_grid() vary, otherwise facet_wrap(). "inline" (ethogram): channel single y-axis row label moves colour legend, channels share one panel. plot_events.default() — callers without anievent. Pass state events first arg point events via point; either may NULL. automatic faceting.

what: Plot a Variable as a Time Series — plot_timeseries
title: Plot a Variable as a Time Series — plot_timeseries
text: Plots one numeric variables aniframe time, one line per trajectory group (every variables_what column every non-time variables_when column metadata). Use per-frame measure — speed, acceleration, confidence score, temperature channel, derived column.

title: Plot a Variable as a Time Series — plot_timeseries
code: plot_timeseries(data, ...)  # Default S3 method plot_timeseries(   data,   variable = NULL,   ...,   layout = c("inline", "facet"),   mode = c("light", "dark"),   palette = "Dark 3" )

title: Plot a Variable as a Time Series — plot_timeseries
text: data aniframe object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused). variable Name(s) column(s) plot y axis — character vector. Required (auto-detection). Several names draw stacked panel per variable. layout Either "inline" (default, groups one panel) "facet" (one panel per group). mode Either "light" (default) "dark"; passed theme_animovement(). palette Name qualitative palette accepted grDevices::hcl.colors(); controls hue family across groups.

title: Plot a Variable as a Time Series — plot_timeseries
text: ggplot object single variable, stacked patchwork several.

title: Plot a Variable as a Time Series — plot_timeseries
text: Pass several names variable draw panel per variable, vertically stacked shared x axis single shared legend (via plots()); stacking makes easy compare shape time. single variable returns plain ggplot. layout argument chooses groups arranged, mirroring plot_events(): "inline" (default): groups share one panel coloured lines, legend one group. "facet": group gets panel — facet_grid() identity (non-time) condition vary, otherwise facet_wrap() panels stacked rows shared x axis lines . Colours come palette_animovement(), matching plot_trajectory(). x axis uses time-unit handling plot_events() — HH:MM:SS true time units, raw frames otherwise.

title: Plot Movement Trajectory — plot_trajectory
text: Creates ggplot x-y trajectory aniframe. One path drawn per trajectory group, group combination every variables_what column every non-time variables_when column aniframe's metadata.

title: Plot Movement Trajectory — plot_trajectory
code: plot_trajectory(data, ...)  # Default S3 method plot_trajectory(data, ..., mode = c("light", "dark"), palette = "Dark 3")

title: Plot Movement Trajectory — plot_trajectory
text: data aniframe object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused). mode Either "light" (default) "dark"; passed theme_animovement(). palette Name qualitative palette accepted grDevices::hcl.colors(); controls hue family across grouping levels.

title: Plot Movement Trajectory — plot_trajectory
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Movement Trajectory — plot_trajectory
text: Colours adapt dataset shape: single trajectory (grouping): line coloured continuously time using Material gradient scale (scale_colour_material_c()), shown time colour bar. --grouping (one varying axis): line gets hue qualitative palette, time mapped alpha line fades start end — shown time legend. matrix grouping (axes vary): line solid, coloured hue × shade matrix palette_animovement() — hue per , shade per ; time reads start/end markers. Every trajectory annotated filled circle first point filled triangle last point, identified start/end legend. Gaps missing data bridged dashed line path stays traceable.

what: Arrange multiple plots side by side — plots
title: Arrange multiple plots side by side — plots
text: thin convenience wrapper around patchwork composing several plots single figure optional sub-figure tags, shared legend, overall title / subtitle / caption. Vendored see package anivis plots can combined without reaching patchwork directly.

title: Arrange multiple plots side by side — plots
code: plots(   ...,   n_rows = NULL,   n_columns = NULL,   guides = NULL,   axes = NULL,   axis_titles = NULL,   tags = FALSE,   tag_prefix = NULL,   tag_suffix = NULL,   tag_sep = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   theme = NULL )

title: Arrange multiple plots side by side — plots
text: ... Plots combine, single list plots. n_rows, n_columns Number rows / columns layout. default patchwork chooses roughly square grid. guides treat legends: "collect" pool identical legends one, "keep" leave place. Passed patchwork::wrap_plots(). axes, axis_titles treat duplicated axes / axis titles: "collect", "collect_x", "collect_y" share across aligned panels, "keep" (default). Passed patchwork::wrap_plots(). tags Sub-figure tags. TRUE labels panels , B, C, ...; character vector c("1", "2") "" chooses another sequence; FALSE (default) adds none. tag_prefix, tag_suffix, tag_sep Strings placed / tag, separator nested tag levels. title, subtitle, caption Overall annotations combined figure. theme ggplot2 theme applied annotations (e.g. title).

title: Arrange multiple plots side by side — plots
text: patchwork object.

title: Arrange multiple plots side by side — plots
code: library(ggplot2) p1 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, wt)) + geom_point() p2 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl))) + geom_bar() plots(p1, p2, tags = TRUE)

title: Okabe-Ito colour scales — scale_okabeito
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, wt, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_okabeito()

title: Animovement ggplot2 Theme — theme_animovement
text: default animovement theme, light dark variants. builds ggplot2::theme_linedraw() — fine black panel border, thin gridlines — swaps linedraw's black facet strips usual light-grey strips dark text, adds plain enlarged title, generous axis-title margins title positioning see::theme_modern(). themes names may added time.

title: Animovement ggplot2 Theme — theme_animovement
code: theme_animovement(mode = c("light", "dark"), base_size = 11, base_family = "")  theme_animovement_light(base_size = 11, base_family = "")  theme_animovement_dark(base_size = 11, base_family = "")

title: Animovement ggplot2 Theme — theme_animovement
text: mode Either "light" (default) "dark". base_size Base font size. base_family Base font family.

title: Animovement ggplot2 Theme — theme_animovement
text: ggplot2 theme object.

what: imputeTS-style Theme for Check Plots — theme_imputets
title: imputeTS-style Theme for Check Plots — theme_imputets
text: larger-font variant theme_animovement() tuned diagnostic check plots (plot.anivis_check_na_timing(), plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize(), plot.anivis_check_confidence()), styled imputeTS package figures adapted . Compared theme_animovement() uses larger base size, places title directly panel (axis), renders subtitle markdown via marquee — words like NA / non-NA can coloured inline .na / .nona classes — puts legend bottom.

title: imputeTS-style Theme for Check Plots — theme_imputets
code: theme_imputets(mode = c("light", "dark"), base_size = 13)

title: imputeTS-style Theme for Check Plots — theme_imputets
text: mode Either "light" (default) "dark". base_size Base font size. Default 13.

title: imputeTS-style Theme for Check Plots — theme_imputets
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: anivis 0.2.0
text: First substantial release plotting layer, built aniframe (>= 0.6.0).

title: anivis 0.2.0
text: plot_trajectory() — x/y paths adaptive colour scheme (hue per , shade per ), time legend, gap bridging, start/end markers. plot_timeseries() — per-frame numeric variable time, inline faceted. plot_events() / plot.anievent() geom_event_state() / geom_event_point() — state point events (ethograms / spike rasters) hms time axis.

title: anivis 0.2.0
text: Presentation methods check_*() objects produced companion anicheck package, dispatched via anivis_check_* class staged shared as_plot_data() generic. plot.anivis_check_na_timing() — missing-value distribution time. plot.anivis_check_na_gapsize() — gap-size occurrence / total bars. plot.anivis_check_confidence() — per-keypoint tracking confidence clipped horizontal violins.

title: anivis 0.2.0
text: theme_animovement() (light/dark) theme_imputets() check plots. Vendored Okabe-Ito Material colour palettes scale_* functions, plots() patchwork wrapper.
